name: Boiled White Icing
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine sugar, water, and corn syrup in a saucepan. Cook, stirring often, until the sugar is dissolved.
2. Bring to a boil and cook until the syrup reaches a soft ball stage (234°F) on a candy thermometer.
3. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites with salt until frothy.
4. Add 1/3 of the boiling syrup to the egg whites in a thin stream, beating constantly.
5. Return the remaining syrup to the heat and cook for a few more minutes.
6. Remove from heat and add the second third of boiling syrup in the same manner.
7. Place the remainder of the syrup on the heat to reach a crack stage (approximately 290°F); add it to the egg whites.
8. Add cream of tartar and vanilla, then whip until creamy.
9. If the icing becomes too stiff and hard, add a few drops of water at a time until it reaches the desired consistency for smooth spreading.
10. Use the icing to cover the top and sides of a chocolate cake or 1, 2, 3, 4 Cake.
